headline: Stripe & Advent Bid $53 Billion to Acquire PayPal amid Stablecoin Push
summary: Payments giant Stripe and private equity firm Advent International have jointly offered to acquire PayPal Holdings for more than $53 billion. This marks one of the largest acquisition deals as both companies are actively expanding into stablecoins, mainly amid the GENIUS Act and the CLARITY Act developments. Ad Ad PayPal Gets $53 Billion Acquisitions Offer

This marks one of the largest acquisition deals as both companies are actively expanding into stablecoins, mainly amid the GENIUS Act and the CLARITY Act developments. headline: Exclusive-Stripe, Advent offer to buy PayPal for more than $53 billion, sources say
summary: Payments company Stripe and private equity firm Advent International have made a joint offer to acquire PayPal Holdings Inc for $60.50 per share, in a deal that would value the payments company at more than $53 billion, two people said.  The offer represents around a 28% premium ‌to PayPal's closing share price on Tuesday.  PayPal, Stripe and Advent declined to comment.

content.title: Exclusive-Stripe, Advent offer to buy PayPal for more than $53 billion, sources say
content.summary: Payments company Stripe and private equity firm Advent International have made a joint offer to acquire PayPal Holdings Inc for $60.50 per share, in a deal that would value the payments company at more than $53 billion, two people said.  The offer represents around a 28% premium ‌to PayPal's closing share price on Tuesday.  PayPal, Stripe and Advent declined to comment.

headline: Does the PayPal takeover deal make any sense?
summary: In today's Opening Bid panel, our guests tackle the Question of the Day: Is PayPal (PYPL) worth the price? Following reports of a potential offer to acquire the struggling payments company, the panel examines whether the stock is an attractive buy. AllSource Investment Management Founder and Managing Partner Patrick Kennedy, HSBC Strategist Duncan Toms, and Yahoo Finance Senior Business Reporter Brooke DiPalma break down the implications of the reported interest and what it could mean for investors.

content.title: Does the PayPal takeover deal make any sense?
content.description:
In today's Opening Bid panel, our guests tackle the Question of the Day: Is PayPal (PYPL) worth the price? Following reports of a potential offer to acquire the struggling payments company, the panel examines whether the stock is an attractive buy.

AllSource Investment Management Founder and Managing Partner Patrick Kennedy, HSBC Strategist Duncan Toms, and Yahoo Finance Senior Business Reporter Brooke DiPalma break down the implications of the reported interest and what it could mean for investors.

headline: Sabre opens travel infrastructure to Silicon Valley developers building the next Voice AI agents powering the complete trip at Bay Area hackathon
summary: Sabre (NASDAQ: SABR) will co-host the DeepLearning.AI Voice AI Hackathon: The Complete Trip on July 18 in Silicon Valley, bringing together developers to explore how agentic travel and voice AI can help remove friction from the end-to-end traveler experience. Sabre pioneered one of the travel industry's first open developer ecosystems, and today it's challenging developers to build agents using its APIs to pull an itinerary into a single conversation, all booked and managed by voice AI.

content.title: Sabre opens travel infrastructure to Silicon Valley developers building the next Voice AI agents powering the complete trip at Bay Area hackathon
content.summary: Sabre (NASDAQ: SABR) will co-host the DeepLearning.AI Voice AI Hackathon: The Complete Trip on July 18 in Silicon Valley, bringing together developers to explore how agentic travel and voice AI can help remove friction from the end-to-end traveler experience. Sabre pioneered one of the travel industry's first open developer ecosystems, and today it's challenging developers to build agents using its APIs to pull an itinerary into a single conversation, all booked and managed by voice AI.

headline: Burry balks at PayPal buyout: "$60.50 is simply too low"
summary: Stripe and private equity giant Advent International have reportedly teamed up to float a massive $53 billion takeover bid for PayPal Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ: PYPL). At $60.50 per share, the joint offer represents a 28% premium to Tuesday’s closing price of $47.37. But for "Big Short" legend Michael Burry, that number isn’t a victory lap—it’s a lowball opening move. headline: Stripe, Advent offer to buy PayPal for more than $53 billion, sources say
summary: Stripe and private equity firm Advent International have made a joint offer to acquire PayPal Holdings Inc for $60.50 per share, in a deal that would value the payments company at more than $53 billion, two people familiar with the matter said.  The offer, submitted earlier this month, is backed by about $50 billion in committed financing from banks, said one of them. ‌The offer represents around a 28% premium to PayPal's closing share price on Tuesday.  PayPal, Stripe and Advent declined to comment.

content.title: Stripe, Advent offer to buy PayPal for more than $53 billion, sources say
content.summary: Stripe and private equity firm Advent International have made a joint offer to acquire PayPal Holdings Inc for $60.50 per share, in a deal that would value the payments company at more than $53 billion, two people familiar with the matter said.  The offer, submitted earlier this month, is backed by about $50 billion in committed financing from banks, said one of them. ‌The offer represents around a 28% premium to PayPal's closing share price on Tuesday.  PayPal, Stripe and Advent declined to comment.

thumbnail.caption: Illustration shows PayPal logo

Make sure to also check out Yahoo Finance's new crypto hub to find the latest crypto-related news.

"The Daily Wolf with Scott Melker" airs every day at 12:00 p.m. Tune in for your daily dose of all things crypto. Make sure to also check out Yahoo Finance's new crypto hub to find the latest crypto-related news.

headline: Is PayPal Holdings (PYPL) Stock Still A Bargain After A $60.50 Bid?
summary: PayPal Holdings has just attracted a high profile US$60.50 per share takeover offer while its stock is still trading well below past peaks. This creates a clear tension between a weak 5 year return record and valuation checks that currently screen the shares as cheap. PayPal Holdings’ share price has fallen about 84.0% over the past 5 years, which means the market has already priced in a major reset in expectations. The headline US$53b bid from Stripe and Advent, together with PayPal’s push...
